title: Adaptive num_modules selection for imported softmax-tail workloads
hypothesis: The earlier imported softmax-tail num_modules result that favored nm1 is now stale because it predates the accepted memory-aware split mapper update; rerunning the nm1 versus nm2 comparison under the newer mapper baseline will show whether module-count choice should remain fixed or be selected adaptively per workload family.

direct_comparison.primary_question: After the accepted mapper update, does the imported softmax-tail benchmark still clearly prefer nm1 over nm2, or has the module-count conclusion become benchmark- or lowering-dependent?
include: the historical num-modules campaign result from runs/campaigns/npu/e2e_eval_onnx_imported_softmax_tail_num_modules_v1
a fresh rerun of the same num-modules campaign under the current mapper baseline
exclude: new benchmark families outside the imported softmax-tail suite
further mapper-policy experiments beyond the already accepted memory-aware split baseline
architecture changes unrelated to module count
follow_on_broad_sweep: only if the refreshed comparison is ambiguous, extend to broader softmax-tail model coverage or additional objective weightings

expected_benefit: replaces an outdated nm1-versus-nm2 conclusion with evidence generated under the current accepted mapper baseline
clarifies whether module-count choice should be fixed or adaptive for softmax-tail workloads
provides a cleaner architecture-level follow-on after the mapper-only memory-aware split proposal
risks: the benchmark remains too narrow to justify a general adaptive policy
the refreshed result may still prefer nm1 strongly, yielding only a closure result rather than a new architecture direction
reusing the old campaign shape may miss workload features needed to expose a true adaptive crossover
